title: AI Model Catalog provider file
description: One file per AI provider: how to talk to it (apiType, auth, apiBase) and what it offers (models with pricing, limits, and evidence-backed capabilities). Consumers must ignore fields they do not recognize.

schemaVersion.description: Semver of the provider-file schema. Major bump = breaking change.

id.description: Stable lowercase identifier. Matches the filename: providers/<id>.json

apiBase.description: Base URL for API calls. Consumers may override locally (e.g. proxies, self-hosted).

apiType.description: Which API shape / client driver to use. Consumers wire one driver per apiType; this field selects it.

auth.description: How to present a credential. The credential itself never appears in the catalog.

scheme.description: bearer = Authorization: Bearer <key>; header = custom header named by 'name'; query = query parameter named by 'name'; none = no credential required.

name.description: Header or query-parameter name when scheme is 'header' or 'query'.

extraHeaders.description: Static headers every request must include (e.g. anthropic-version).

lastChecked.description: UTC ISO 8601 timestamp of the last successful updater run for this provider.

providerModelId.description: Exact model identifier to send to the provider API, when it differs from id.

costClass.description: See docs/FREE-PAID-CLASSIFICATION.md. Free does not mean unlimited.

capabilities.description: Keys from docs/CAPABILITY-FLAGS.md. PUBLISHED ONLY when verified by a live catalog test (source tested_by_catalog). Empty until tested.

hints.description: Provider-declared capability flags from the model-list API, kept only to decide what to live-test. NOT verified — never treat as confirmed. Same shape as capabilities.

source.description: Where this model entry came from, e.g. openrouter_models_api.

value.description: Never present guessed data as confirmed. See docs/CAPABILITY-FLAGS.md.
